title: See, Plan, Cut: MPC-Based Autonomous Volumetric Robotic Laser Surgery with OCT Guidance
subtitle: Closed-loop volumetric laser resection with OCT guidance via sampling-based MPC.
affiliationsLine: Duke University • Mechanical Engineering & Materials Science • School of Medicine • Neurosurgery

teaser.alt: RATS platform and OCT-guided volumetric resection
teaser.caption: RATS: OCT-guided robotic laser surgery with constraint-aware volumetric planning.

abstract: Robotic laser systems offer the potential for sub-millimeter, non-contact, high-precision tissue resection, yet existing platforms lack volumetric planning and intraoperative feedback. We present RATS (Robot-Assisted Tissue Surgery), an intelligent opto-mechanical, OCT-guided robotic platform designed for autonomous volumetric soft tissue resection in surgical applications. RATS integrates macro-scale RGB-D imaging, micro-scale OCT, and a fiber-coupled surgical laser, calibrated through a multistage alignment pipeline. A super-Gaussian LTI model characterizes ablation crater morphology, and a sampling-based MPC framework operates directly on OCT voxel data for closed-loop, constraint-aware resection, including preservation of subsurface structures.
contributions: An OCT-guided opto-mechanical robotic platform for volumetric laser resection.
Multi-stage calibration aligning OCT, laser, and robot frames for accurate targeting.
Super-Gaussian laser–tissue interaction model validated with OCT crater reconstructions.
Sampling-based MPC planner operating directly on OCT voxels for closed-loop execution.
